notes:
Khỏe means healthy or in good physical condition. It can also mean strong (physically) or simply feeling well.

It’s an adjective, used after the subject: Tôi khỏe = “I’m healthy / I’m fine.” It describes people most often, but also body parts (tim khỏe = strong heart).

You can compare it: khỏe hơn (healthier/stronger), khỏe nhất (healthiest/strongest).  

Strong opposite: yếu (weak). Close friend word: khỏe mạnh = healthy and strong (a bit more formal/complete).
exampleSentences: Buổi sáng anh thường chạy bộ để giữ cho mình luôn khỏe.
Chị ăn nhiều rau và trái cây để cơ thể khỏe hơn.
Mỗi khi em cảm thấy khỏe, em thích đi chơi với bạn bè.

definitions.vi: Khỏe mạnh; có sức khỏe tốt.

example.vi: Tôi cảm thấy khỏe hôm nay.

exampleSentencesTranslated: In the morning, I usually jog to stay healthy.
She eats a lot of vegetables and fruit to be more healthy.
Whenever I feel healthy, I like to hang out with my friends.
notes_md:
**Khỏe** means *healthy* or *in good physical condition*. It can also mean *strong* (physically) or simply *feeling well*.

It’s an adjective, used after the subject: *Tôi khỏe* = “I’m healthy / I’m fine.” It describes people most often, but also body parts (*tim khỏe* = strong heart).

You can compare it: **khỏe hơn** (healthier/stronger), **khỏe nhất** (healthiest/strongest).  

Strong opposite: **yếu** (weak). Close friend word: **khỏe mạnh** = healthy and strong (a bit more formal/complete).
